title: Apply for the Chafee Grant
description: Get up to ~$5,000/year for college or job training — free money you don't pay back, made specifically for foster youth. Most kids never claim it.

steps.title: Check that you qualify
steps.what: You generally qualify if you were in foster care for at least one day between ages 16 and 18 and you're not yet 26.
steps.why: Tons of eligible foster youth miss this purely because nobody told them they qualify.
steps.action: Confirm your foster dates. Not sure? Ask me or your ILP/social worker.
steps.ai_check: Do you think you meet the age 16-18 in-care rule?

steps.title: File your FAFSA first
steps.what: The Chafee Grant needs your FAFSA on file. As a former foster youth you file as an 'independent' student — no parent info needed.
steps.why: FAFSA also unlocks Pell Grants and tuition waivers. It's the master key.
steps.action: Go to studentaid.gov and complete this year's FAFSA. We can do it step by step.
steps.ai_check: Is your FAFSA submitted for this year?

steps.title: Submit the Chafee Grant application
steps.what: In California it's a short separate application through the Student Aid Commission (CSAC).
steps.why: This is the actual money request. FAFSA alone doesn't trigger it.
steps.action: Apply at csac.ca.gov (search 'Chafee Grant'). It takes about 15 minutes.
steps.ai_check: Did you submit the Chafee application at csac.ca.gov?

steps.title: Pick an eligible school or training program
steps.what: The grant works at colleges and many vocational/training programs that take at least one year to complete.
steps.why: Choosing an eligible program is what lets the money actually pay out.
steps.action: Enroll (or confirm enrollment) at an eligible school. Ask me to check if yours qualifies.
steps.ai_check: Are you enrolled in an eligible school or program?

steps.title: Send proof and track your award
steps.what: Your school's financial aid office verifies your enrollment so the grant pays out, often split across terms.
steps.why: A 5-minute follow-up with financial aid is the difference between approved and paid.
steps.action: Email or visit your financial aid office and confirm they have your Chafee award. Re-apply each year.
steps.ai_check: Has your financial aid office confirmed your Chafee award?
